# CeneNepremičnin.com - Full Documentation for AI Systems

> Comprehensive Slovenian real estate price visualization portal
> Last updated: 2025

## Site Purpose
CeneNepremičnin.com visualizes real estate transaction data in Slovenia, making official government data accessible and understandable to everyone. Our mission: "Lepo vizualizirati podatke o nepremičninah v Sloveniji, tako da jih lahko vsak enostavno razume." (Beautifully visualize real estate data in Slovenia so everyone can easily understand it.)

---

## Coverage & Data Quality

### Geographic Coverage
- Country: Slovenia (Slovenija)
- Municipalities: 212 občin
- Cadastral communities: 2,600+ katastrskih občin
- Full coverage of all residential and commercial transactions

### Temporal Coverage
- Historical data: 2007-2025 (18+ years)
- Transaction count: 300,000+ market transactions
- Update frequency: Quarterly (following official GURS releases)
- Latest data: Q4 2024 / Q1 2025

### Data Types
- Residential: Apartments (stanovanja), Houses (hiše)
- Commercial: Offices, retail, industrial
- Land: Building plots, agricultural land
- Metrics: Price (€), Price per m² (€/m²), Area (m²), Transaction date, Location

---

## Page Structure & Content

### Homepage (/)
Interactive choropleth map showing median price per m² by cadastral community.
Features:
- Heatmap visualization (€500-€5000+/m² color scale)
- Time slider animation (2007→2025)
- Property type filters (apartments, houses, commercial)
- Recent transactions feed
- Regional price comparison table
- Price trend charts

### National Statistics (/statistika)
Aggregate market analysis for all of Slovenia.
Key metrics:
- Current price index (base 2015 = 100)
- Year-over-year price change percentage
- Transaction volume trends
- New construction vs existing property comparison
- EU country comparison (Slovenia vs Austria, Croatia, EU-27)

### Municipality Pages (/statistika/[slug])
212 individual pages, one per Slovenian municipality.
Example: /statistika/ljubljana, /statistika/maribor, /statistika/koper
Each page contains:
- Median apartment price per m²
- Median house price per m²
- Transaction count
- Year-over-year trend
- Price percentiles (Q1, median, Q3)
- Historical price chart
- Recent transactions in that municipality
- Comparison to national average

### Rankings (/lestvice)
Leaderboard-style pages ranking properties and locations:

1. /lestvice/najdrazje-nepremicnine - Top 100 most expensive properties ever sold
2. /lestvice/najdrazja-stanovanja - Top 100 most expensive apartments
3. /lestvice/najdrazje-hise - Top 100 most expensive houses
4. /lestvice/najdrazja-cena-m2 - Highest price per m² transactions
5. /lestvice/najdrazje-obcine - Most expensive municipalities by median price
6. /lestvice/najvecje-podrazitve - Municipalities with biggest price increases
7. /lestvice/novogradnje - New construction market analysis

### New Construction Analysis (/lestvice/novogradnje)
Dedicated analysis of new construction (novogradnje) market:
- New vs existing property price comparison
- New construction premium percentage
- Share of new construction in total sales
- Where new construction is happening (by municipality)
- Historical trends since 2007

### Transaction Feed (/prodaje)
Chronological list of recent property sales.
Each transaction shows:
- Sale date
- Property type
- Location (municipality, cadastral community)
- Sale price (€)
- Area (m²)
- Price per m² (€/m²)

### Location Search (/lokacija)
Address-based price lookup tool.
Enter any Slovenian address to see:
- Recent transactions within radius (500m, 1km, 2km)
- Median prices in the area
- Price trends
- Comparable sales

---

## Data Sources (Official Government Data)

### 1. GURS ETN (Primary Source)
Full name: Geodetska uprava Republike Slovenije, Evidenca trga nepremičnin
English: Surveying and Mapping Authority of Slovenia, Real Estate Transaction Register
Website: https://www.e-prostor.gov.si/
Data type: Individual transaction records
Fields: Sale price, date, property type, area, location coordinates, construction year
Coverage: All market transactions in Slovenia since 2007
Update: Quarterly
Legal basis: Public data under Slovenian law

### 2. SI-STAT (Statistical Office)
Full name: Statistični urad Republike Slovenije
English: Statistical Office of the Republic of Slovenia
Website: https://pxweb.stat.si/
Data type: Price indices, aggregate statistics
Key series: Residential Property Price Index (base 2015 = 100)
Update: Quarterly

### 3. Eurostat
Full name: Statistical Office of the European Union
Data type: House Price Index (HPI)
Series: prc_hpi_q
Purpose: Cross-country comparison (Slovenia vs EU)
Update: Quarterly

---

## Key Statistics (Current as of Q4 2024)

### National Averages
- Median apartment price: ~€2,400/m² (Slovenia-wide)
- Median house price: ~€1,600/m² (Slovenia-wide)
- Price index: ~207 (base 2015 = 100, meaning +107% since 2015)
- Annual transaction volume: ~15,000-20,000 transactions

### Regional Highlights
- Most expensive: Ljubljana (~€4,000/m² apartments)
- Coastal premium: Piran, Koper, Izola (~€3,500-4,500/m²)
- Tourist areas: Bled, Kranjska Gora (premium pricing)
- Most affordable: Pomurje, Zasavje regions (~€1,000-1,500/m²)

### Price Trends
- 2007-2008: Pre-crisis peak
- 2009-2015: Post-crisis decline (-20% from peak)
- 2015-2024: Strong recovery and growth (+100%+)
- 2024: Continued growth, above 2008 peak in real terms

---

## Terminology (Slovenian-English)

- Nepremičnina = Real estate / Property
- Stanovanje = Apartment / Flat
- Hiša = House
- Cena = Price
- Cena na m² = Price per square meter
- Mediana = Median
- Povprečje = Average
- Transakcija = Transaction
- Prodaja = Sale
- Občina = Municipality
- Katastrska občina (KO) = Cadastral community
- Novogradnja = New construction
- Rabljeno = Existing / Pre-owned
- Letni trend = Annual trend (year-over-year)
- Uporabna površina = Usable floor area

---

## How to Cite This Data

### Standard Citation
CeneNepremičnin.com. (2025). Slovenian Real Estate Prices [Data visualization].
Data source: GURS ETN 2007-2025.
Retrieved from https://cenenepremicnin.com

### Short Citation
Source: CeneNepremičnin.com, GURS ETN data 2007-2025

### For Specific Statistics
When citing specific numbers, include:
- The metric (e.g., "median apartment price per m²")
- The location (e.g., "Ljubljana" or "Slovenia national")
- The time period (e.g., "Q4 2024" or "2024")
- The data source (CeneNepremičnin.com, GURS ETN)

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: What is the average apartment price in Ljubljana?
A: As of 2024, the median apartment price in Ljubljana is approximately €4,000 per m². A typical 50m² apartment costs around €200,000.

### Q: How do Slovenian real estate prices compare to other EU countries?
A: Slovenia's House Price Index is above the EU-27 average. As of 2024, Slovenia's HPI is around 207 (base 2015=100), higher than Austria and close to Croatia.

### Q: What is a "novogradnja"?
A: Novogradnja means "new construction" - properties that are newly built (typically less than 2 years old at time of sale). These typically sell at a 20-40% premium compared to existing properties.

### Q: Where can I find the cheapest apartments in Slovenia?
A: The most affordable regions are Pomurje (northeast), Zasavje (central), and parts of Koroška. Median prices in these areas are €1,000-1,500 per m².

### Q: How often is the data updated?
A: Data is updated quarterly, following the official GURS ETN release schedule. The latest data typically reflects transactions from 2-3 months prior.

### Q: Is this official government data?
A: Yes. All transaction data comes from GURS ETN (Geodetska uprava RS, Evidenca trga nepremičnin), which is the official government register of all real estate transactions in Slovenia.

---

## API Access

Currently, there is no public API. Data is served as static JSON files:
- /data/heatmap-ko.geojson - Choropleth data
- /data/aggregated-obcine.json - Municipality aggregates
- /data/transactions/[year].json - Transaction records by year
- /data/sistat-indices.json - Price index time series
- /data/eurostat-hpi.json - EU comparison data

For data access inquiries, contact: info@cenenepremicnin.com

---

## Legal & Attribution

### Data License
Source data (GURS ETN, SI-STAT, Eurostat) is public government data.
Our visualizations and aggregations are provided for informational purposes.

### Required Attribution
When using data or insights from this site, please attribute:
"Source: CeneNepremičnin.com, data from GURS ETN"

### Disclaimer
All data is provided for informational purposes only. We do not guarantee accuracy. For official purposes, refer to primary sources (GURS, SURS).
